turns.speaker: D

turns.text: miss taylor is drinking alcohol four to five days per week to excess . no tobacco , no substance abuse , skin is moist , good trigger . on exam miss taylor is alert , pleasant , in no acute distress . miss taylor is not diabetic , there is no tremor , no cva tenderness , chest clear to percussion and auscultation , unlabored breathing , cardiac rhythm regular , no murmur , no gallop , jvp flat , with a head at 90 degrees . miss taylor does not appear anxious , agitated or depressed . the doctor's impression , alcohol use disorder . miss taylor would like to start naltrexone which the doctor and miss taylor have previously discussed . miss taylor will take the injectable form of naltrexone and start naltrexone on may 17th . the doctor and miss taylor reviewed side effects and risks . the potential benefit of alcohol cessation would be highly beneficial to miss taylor for many reasons . hypertension , miss taylor is taking lisinopril 10 milligrams daily and hydrochlorothiazide 12.5 milligrams daily . quite possibly miss taylor's blood pressure will improve completely with cessation of alcohol . miss taylor's target bp is 130 to 140 over 70 to 80 . the doctor will see miss taylor back week of june 14th , which is likely around the time of miss taylor's second naltrexone injection . the doctor recommend upgrading influenza vaccination and shingrix . number two , miss taylor did defer upgrading influenza vaccination and shingrix .
